narrative: Dear [Student's Name], as a Black woman applying to law school, you've shown impressive determination in pursuing your dream of becoming a lawyer, and we appreciate the strengths in your profile, like your solid undergraduate GPA of 3.30—which is above the typical student's 3.21—and your strong LSAT score of 40, higher than the average of 36.70. Your placement in the 9th decile for both Year 1 and Year 3 grades reflects top performance in your school, and coming from an upper-middle income family positions you well in many ways, especially as a full-time student like most applicants. However, the admission model, which predicts the likelihood of passing the bar exam on the first try, estimated your risk of failure at 59.6%, tipping the scales just over the threshold that led to your application's rejection. This prediction was driven primarily by your undergraduate GPA, which, despite being solid, stood out as the biggest factor pushing the model toward forecasting a higher chance of bar failure compared to what it expects from successful passers. On a more positive note, your Year 3 decile ranking was the strongest counterforce, strongly pulling the prediction back toward expecting you to pass, as it signals excellent late-stage academic momentum. Your LSAT score and family income level each played moderate roles in nudging the outlook slightly toward potential failure, while your full-time status and Year 1 decile had minimal sway. Overall, compared to typical students who pass the bar, your profile is competitive in LSAT, income, and deciles, but the model weighed your GPA as the key shortfall against patterns it has learned from past bar-takers. This isn't a judgment on your potential as a Black woman trailblazer in law—many with similar backgrounds overcome these metrics through targeted growth. To move forward, consider retaking the LSAT or bolstering your GPA narrative with explanations of any challenges, perhaps through additional coursework or a post-bac program, and reapply to schools with holistic reviews that value your unique story. We're rooting for you to rewrite this prediction and join the ranks of successful bar passers. Keep pushing forward.
